text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- PROTOCOL FOLLOW-UP VISIT / ON-TRIAL ASSESSMENT NOTE

ATTENDING: Thoracic Oncology / Medical Oncology
VISIT TYPE: On-Trial Protocol Visit (Phase II Investigator-Sponsored Trial -- Savolitinib + osi)
VISIT NUMBER: Approximately 6-Month On-Trial Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 65-year-old woman with Stage IV (M1b, brain metastases) EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, presenting today for her protocol-specified 6-month on-trial assessment visit. She is enrolled on a Phase II single-arm investigator-sponsored clinical trial of savolitinib 300 mg daily plus Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily for MET-amplified EGFR-mutant NSCLC following progression on third-generation EGFR TKI therapy. She is here for evaluation of disease status, toxicity assessment, collection of protocol-required samples, and continuation of trial medications.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Yuen's oncologic history is well-known. Briefly, she was diagnosed approximately two years ago with Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe, confirmed on CT-guided core biopsy. Initial FoundationOne CDx demonstrated EGFR L858R (exon 21, allele frequency 38.2%), co-mutant TP53 R248W, and low-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 2.4) at baseline. PD-L1 TPS was 5% by 22C3. She was treated with Tagrisso 80 mg daily as first-line therapy (not a surgical candidate given N3 nodal disease at diagnosis), achieving an excellent initial partial response -- the primary mass shrank from 5.8 cm to approximately 1.8 cm by 12 months, with near-complete nodal response.

She developed disease progression at approximately 14 months on osi, manifesting as worsening headaches and mild word-finding difficulties. MRI at that time demonstrated three new intracranial metastases (left frontal 12 mm, right cerebellum 8 mm, left parietal 6 mm), and concurrent CT chest showed growth of the right upper lobe mass to 2.7 cm with a new right paratracheal lymph node. Liquid biopsy (Guardant360) identified an acquired EGFR T790M (allele frequency 0.6%, atypically low for Tagrisso failure) alongside EGFR L858R. Tissue re-biopsy with FoundationOne CDx re-profiling revealed a complex, heterogeneous resistance landscape including: low-level T790M (3.1%), newly acquired PIK3CA E545K and PTEN R130Q, and critically, high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.1). The molecular tumor board identified MET amplification as the likely dominant driver of resistance.

She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to all three brain metastases approximately 6 weeks after progression was identified, with excellent neurological recovery. Following SRS, she received four cycles of bridging carbo (AUC 5) plus peme every 21 days, achieving a partial response in the chest and stable/responding CNS disease. She was then enrolled on the current Phase II trial of savolitinib plus Tagrisso, which she initiated approximately 6 months ago.

Since enrolling on trial, she has had one protocol-specified restaging at 8 weeks, which showed continued partial response -- the right upper lobe residual mass decreased from 2.1 cm (at trial entry) to 1.4 cm, with further shrinkage or resolution of the intracranial lesions. The left parietal metastasis was no longer visualized at 8 weeks; the left frontal lesion had decreased to 4 mm; the right cerebellar lesion was stable at 4 mm.

Today's visit corresponds to the 6-month on-trial restaging assessment. Most recent imaging (reviewed below) continues to show partial response/stable disease without evidence of new progression.

She reports the following interim symptom update since her last visit approximately 8 weeks ago:
- Fatigue: Mild, grade 1, present but stable. She describes it as a "low-level background tiredness" that is manageable and has not worsened.
- Peripheral edema: Previously grade 2, now grade 1 and well-controlled on furosemide 20 mg daily. She is wearing compression stockings consistently. No new swelling in hands or face.
- Acneiform rash: Grade 1 facial papulopustular rash, stable, managed with topical clindamycin. She finds it cosmetically bothersome but tolerable.
- Nausea: Grade 1, intermittent, managed without standing antiemetics. She takes ginger tea and eats small frequent meals.
- Neurological: Headaches have fully resolved since SRS and completion of dexamethasone taper. No word-finding difficulties. No new focal deficits, no vision changes, no seizures, no gait difficulties.
- Respiratory: Cough is minimal (dry, occasional), significantly improved from her presentation. No dyspnea at rest. Mild exertional dyspnea with vigorous activity, stable.
- Appetite / weight: Appetite has improved compared to during chemotherapy. She reports eating reasonably well. Weight is stable (reviewed below).
- Mood / quality of life: She reports improved mood and overall quality of life compared to the period around progression. She has returned to part-time teaching (3 days per week) and is finding that meaningful and energizing. She walks 20-30 minutes most days.

No chest pain, no hemoptysis, no bone pain, no new lymphadenopathy noted by the patient, no abdominal pain, no jaundice.

Medication adherence: She confirms taking savolitinib 300 mg orally each morning and Tagrisso 80 mg orally each morning without interruption since last visit. No missed doses reported. Furosemide 20 mg daily taken consistently. She takes folic acid 400 mcg daily (residual from prior Alimta protocol; continued as general supplement).

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1 as above. No fever, no chills, no night sweats. Weight stable per patient.
- HEENT: No headache (resolved). No vision changes. No epistaxis.
- Cardiovascular: Bilateral lower extremity edema grade 1, stable on diuretic. No palpitations, no chest pain, no orthopnea, no paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. Prior echocardiogram (obtained at time of edema onset on trial) showed normal LV function with EF 60-65%, no pericardial effusion.
- Respiratory: Mild dry cough, stable. Mild exertional dyspnea, stable. No pleuritic chest pain.
- GI: Intermittent mild nausea, grade 1. Loose stools 1-2 per day above baseline (grade 1 diarrhea, consistent with osi class effect, unchanged). No blood per rectum. No abdominal pain.
- GU: Normal urinary frequency. No hematuria.
- Musculoskeletal: No bone pain, no myalgias, no arthralgias.
- Neurological: Fully intact. No headaches, no focal deficits, no cognitive symptoms, no gait instability. Resolved anomic aphasia (was present at initial intracranial progression; fully recovered).
- Skin: Grade 1 facial acneiform rash, perinasal and forehead distribution, stable. Grade 1 paronychia of right index finger, mild and intermittent.
- Hematologic: No bruising, no bleeding, no significant infections since last visit.
- Endocrine/metabolic: No symptoms of hypothyroidism. No glucose intolerance reported.
- Psychiatric: Mood is good. Mild anxiety related to oncologic uncertainty, but patient describes this as manageable and appropriate. She follows up with her primary care physician and has access to social work support through the cancer center.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: (Previously documented; summarized)
- Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, as above
- Chronic small vessel ischemic white matter changes (noted on brain MRI, clinically silent)
- Former smoker, 12 pack-year history, quit approximately 20 years ago
- No prior cardiac history; echo as noted above normal

SURGICAL / PROCEDURAL HISTORY:
- CT-guided transthoracic core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass (initial diagnosis)
- CT-guided core needle re-biopsy, right upper lobe mass and right paratracheal lymph node (at progression)
- Stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife Icon), 3 brain metastases (approximately 16 months ago)

MEDICATIONS:
1. Savolitinib 300 mg orally once daily (Phase II trial drug, MET inhibitor)
2. Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily (Phase II trial drug, 3rd-generation EGFR TKI)
3. Furosemide 20 mg orally once daily (for savolitinib-related peripheral edema)
4. Folic acid 400 mcg orally once daily
5. Topical clindamycin 1% solution to face twice daily (for acneiform rash)
6. Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg as needed (headache, now rarely used)
7. Ondansetron 8 mg orally as needed (for nausea, occasional use)

ALLERGIES: NKDA (No Known Drug Allergies)

SOCIAL HISTORY: (Previously documented)
- Born in Hong Kong; immigrated to US at age 32
- High school math teacher; currently working part-time (3 days/week), finds it meaningful
- Married; supportive spouse present at today's visit
- Former smoker, 12 pack-year history, quit ~20 years ago
- Occasional glass of wine socially; no recreational drug use
- Lives at home with husband; two adult children, supportive family

FAMILY HISTORY: Paternal uncle with lung cancer (deceased); no other known malignancies.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Pleasant, well-appearing 65-year-old woman in no acute distress. Alert, oriented, conversant. No acute distress. Appears her stated age.
Vitals: Blood pressure 118/72 mmHg, heart rate 74 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, temperature 98.4°F (36.9°C), oxygen saturation 97% on room air. Weight 57.8 kg (stable from last visit; has regained weight since initial diagnosis; at diagnosis she had lost 8 lbs but has maintained recovery). BMI 22.4 kg/m2.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL, EOMI. Sclerae anicteric. Mild papulopustular rash over forehead and nasolabial folds, consistent with EGFR TKI acneiform rash, grade 1. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis.
Neck: Supple. No palpable lymphadenopathy in cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary regions. Previously palpable right supraclavicular node is no longer present on today's exam (has been absent since early response to Tagrisso).
Chest/Respiratory: Effort is normal. Breath sounds are clear bilaterally. No wheezing, rales, or rhonchi. No dullness to percussion. No pleural rub. Previously noted decreased breath sounds at the right apex are minimally present today.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1, S2. No S3, no S4. No murmurs. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. Bowel sounds normal.
Extremities: Bilateral 1+ pitting edema to the mid-ankle level (previously mid-shin at peak, now improved to ankles). No calf tenderness. Peripheral pulses intact bilaterally.
Skin: Grade 1 acneiform rash face as described. Grade 1 paronychia right index finger (mild periungual erythema). No other skin lesions.
Neurological: Alert and fully oriented x4 (person, place, time, situation). Speech fluent, no aphasia (previously had anomic aphasia during CNS progression; fully resolved). Cranial nerves II-XII intact on screening. Strength 5/5 in all four extremities, no drift. Sensation grossly intact. Coordination: Finger-nose-finger intact, no dysmetria, no ataxia. Gait steady, normal cadence. Romberg negative.
Lymphatics: No palpable lymphadenopathy in any regional chain (neck, supraclavicular, axillary, inguinal).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
(Reviewed from today's protocol-specified laboratory draws)
CBC:
- WBC: 6.1 x 10^9/L (normal)
- ANC: 3.8 x 10^9/L (normal; no evidence of myelosuppression)
- Hemoglobin: 11.9 g/dL (mild anemia, stable; consistent with chronic disease/prior platinum exposure)
- Platelets: 198 x 10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 139 mEq/L
- Potassium: 3.7 mEq/L (low-normal in context of furosemide use; recommend monitoring)
- Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL; eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2
- BUN: 14 mg/dL
- Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL
- AST: 28 U/L
- ALT: 31 U/L
- Alkaline phosphatase: 72 U/L
- Albumin: 3.9 g/dL
- Glucose: 96 mg/dL

LFTs are within normal limits, reassuring for absence of hepatotoxicity from savolitinib or Tagrisso. No evidence of renal dysfunction.

Thyroid function: TSH 2.1 mIU/L (normal; checked given prior Tagrisso use and interest in immune-related endocrinopathy surveillance on trial).

Protocol-specified ctDNA (Guardant360, blood draw today -- results pending at time of visit; preliminary verbal report from laboratory -- EGFR L858R allele frequency approximately 0.3%, MET amplification below detection threshold): This represents a continued favorable molecular response compared to baseline at trial entry, where EGFR L858R allele frequency was 1.2% on liquid biopsy. MET amplification was high-level at tissue re-biopsy but appears below threshold on ctDNA at the 6-month mark, which is encouraging and potentially consistent with deep molecular suppression of the dominant resistant clone by savolitinib.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Today's protocol-specified restaging scans (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast and MRI brain with and without gadolinium) reviewed in full:

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis: The right upper lobe residual mass now measures approximately 1.3 cm, stable compared to 1.4 cm at the 8-week on-trial scan. No interval change. There is no new mediastinal or hilar adenopathy; previously enlarged right paratracheal node is no longer measurable. No new lung nodules, consolidation, or pleural effusion. Liver and adrenal glands are unremarkable. No new hepatic lesions. No new osseous lesions identified on the axial images reviewed. No new abdominal or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

MRI Brain with and without gadolinium: The previously treated left frontal lobe metastasis now measures approximately 2 mm, essentially at the limit of detection -- this lesion has gone from 12 mm at time of progression, to 7 mm post-SRS, to 4 mm at 8-week on-trial scan, and is now nearly resolved. The right cerebellar hemisphere lesion is stable at 4 mm, compatible with treated/involuting metastasis (no surrounding edema, no contrast enhancement pattern suggestive of active disease). The left parietal lesion continues to be absent -- not visualized on this or the prior 8-week scan, suggesting complete response at that site. No new intracranial enhancing lesions. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No mass effect. White matter T2/FLAIR changes are stable and consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic changes, unchanged.

Overall imaging impression: Partial response in chest maintained (right upper lobe mass 1.3 cm, down from 5.8 cm at original diagnosis -- a reduction of approximately 78% from baseline). CNS disease showing continued response/near-resolution at previously treated sites. No evidence of systemic or intracranial progression at this 6-month on-trial assessment.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. Stage IV (M1b, intracranial metastases) EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, currently on Phase II clinical trial of savolitinib 300 mg daily plus Tagrisso 80 mg daily.

Ms. Yuen continues to demonstrate a maintained partial response at her 6-month on-trial assessment. The primary residual right upper lobe mass is stable at 1.3 cm -- representing a sustained and durable response from her original 5.8 cm tumor at diagnosis. From the perspective of the dominant MET-amplified resistant clone that drove her osi failure, the molecular data (ctDNA MET amplification now below detection threshold, declining EGFR L858R signal) is encouraging and suggests meaningful suppression of the resistant subclone by savolitinib in combination with osi rechallenge. The PIK3CA/PTEN co-alterations identified at re-biopsy continue to be a point of molecular interest; their contribution to ongoing response or potential future resistance will be monitored on protocol. The CNS picture is excellent -- three previously treated brain metastases continue to show sustained response or resolution.

Plan:
- Continue savolitinib 300 mg daily plus osi 80 mg daily at current doses. No dose modification indicated.
- Protocol compliance confirmed; trial drug supply refilled for the next cycle period.
- ctDNA results (Guardant360 from today) to be formally reported through the laboratory; results will be communicated to patient and documented in the protocol response assessment.
- Correlative protocol samples (PBMC, serum banking) collected per study protocol today.

2. CNS metastases, status post stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to three lesions, with excellent response.

All three previously treated lesions are showing continued involution or resolution on today's MRI. No new intracranial lesions. No symptoms of intracranial disease -- headaches resolved, no neurological deficits, no aphasia recurrence. No evidence of radiation necrosis at any of the three treated sites (lesions are shrinking, not expanding, with decreasing or absent enhancement). Dexamethasone was tapered off and discontinued at prior visit; she is not on corticosteroids currently.

Plan:
- Continue MRI brain monitoring per protocol schedule (next 8-week restaging scan).
- No additional radiation therapy or neurosurgical intervention planned at this time.
- She should report any new or worsening headaches, visual symptoms, focal deficits, or seizures immediately.

3. Savolitinib-related peripheral edema, grade 1 on furosemide.

Edema has improved from grade 2 (mid-shin, pitting) at 4 weeks on trial to grade 1 (ankle-level) with furosemide 20 mg daily. She is wearing compression stockings. Electrolytes are acceptable; potassium is low-normal at 3.7 mEq/L in context of furosemide -- will supplement orally (potassium chloride 20 mEq daily added) and recheck at next visit. Echocardiogram previously normal.

Plan:
- Continue furosemide 20 mg daily.
- Add potassium chloride 20 mEq orally daily; recheck BMP in 2-4 weeks.
- Continue compression stockings.
- Repeat echocardiogram not indicated at this time (last echo was normal, no new cardiac symptoms).

4. EGFR TKI-class acneiform rash, grade 1 -- stable.

Facial papulopustular rash grade 1, present at current level since starting Tagrisso rechallenge on trial. Managed with topical clindamycin 1% solution twice daily and urea-based moisturizer. Grade 1 paronychia right index finger, intermittent. No secondary infection.

Plan:
- Continue topical clindamycin 1% solution to face.
- Continue moisturizer regimen.
- Patient aware to contact clinic if rash progresses to grade 2 (pustular crusting, denuded skin, significant discomfort) -- would reassess for potential oral doxycycline and/or dose interruption if needed.

5. Fatigue, grade 1 -- stable.

Likely multifactorial: systemic therapy-related (savolitinib + osi combination, prior platinum exposure), mild anemia (hemoglobin 11.9 g/dL, stable), and the psychological burden of a serious diagnosis and ongoing treatment. She is managing this with pacing, part-time work, daily walking, and good social support.

Plan:
- Continue current management: pacing, moderate exercise as tolerated, adequate nutrition.
- Hemoglobin stable -- no transfusion or erythropoiesis-stimulating agent indicated at this time.
- Encourage continued physical activity.

6. Mild anemia, hemoglobin 11.9 g/dL.

Stable, mild, consistent with anemia of chronic disease and cumulative effect of prior platinum-based chemotherapy. No acute blood loss. Iron studies to be checked at next visit to rule out iron deficiency component.

Plan:
- Iron studies (serum iron, ferritin, TIBC) at next protocol visit.
- No transfusion indicated.

7. Performance status and quality of life.

ECOG performance status today is 1. She is active, working part-time, ambulatory, and self-caring without limitation. Quality of life has improved substantially since the difficult period of Tagrisso progression and transition through SRS and chemotherapy. Her return to teaching is a meaningful marker of functional recovery. She expressed gratitude for the clinical trial opportunity and remains highly engaged in her care.

Plan:
- Continue QOL monitoring per protocol PRO instruments (completed today).
- Continue to facilitate social work, patient navigation, and psychological support resources as needed.

8. Next steps / follow-up:

- Next protocol visit and restaging scans (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis + MRI brain): approximately 8 weeks.
- Recheck BMP in 2-4 weeks by phone/portal to review potassium after supplementation initiation.
- Formal ctDNA report (today's Guardant360) to be communicated when available.
- Continued communication with molecular tumor board regarding ongoing monitoring of PIK3CA/PTEN co-alteration trajectory.
- If disease remains stable/responding at next restaging, will discuss with PI and sponsor whether any protocol modification or biomarker analysis is warranted.
- Patient has been counseled to call with any new symptoms, particularly neurological symptoms, hemoptysis, or new bony pain.

Total time with patient today: approximately 55 minutes (including protocol assessments and PRO instrument completion). All questions answered. Patient verbalized understanding.

Cancer is present (residual disease), responding to therapy. Patient remains on active Phase II clinical trial protocol in ongoing partial response at 6 months.

---
Electronically signed: Attending Physician, Medical Oncology / Thoracic Oncology
CC: Primary Care Physician, Radiation Oncology (for ongoing CNS surveillance co-management), Trial PI/Sponsor
